title: Summary: Joint Health Canada and United States Occupational Safety and Health Administration Guidance - Canada.ca
content:
## Summary: Joint Health Canada and United States Occupational Safety and Health Administration Guidance
- Hazardous Products Act
- Hazardous Products Regulations
This guidance is intended to communicate information related to both Canada and the United States (U.S.) labelling requirements for hazardous products under the Canadian Hazardous Products Regulations (HPR) and the U.S. Hazard Communication Standard (HCS) 2012.

## Who this guide is for
- Industries who sell or import hazardous chemicals
- 4. Policy Objectives
- 6. Joint Occupational Safety and Health Administration/Health Canada Guidance on Regulatory Processes for Hazardous Products in the Workplace 6.1 Comparison of United States (U.S.) and Canada's Regulatory Process for Hazardous Products in the Workplace
- 6.1 Comparison of United States (U.S.) and Canada's Regulatory Process for Hazardous Products in the Workplace
- 7. Joint Occupational Safety and Health Administration/Health Canada Comparison of Labelling Requirements for Hazardous Products 7.1 Label Comparison for Shipped Containers
- 7.1 Label Comparison for Shipped Containers
- 8. Joint Occupational Safety and Health Administration/Health Canada Guidance on Labelling Pictogram for Hazards Not Otherwise Classified, Physical Hazards Not Otherwise Classified and Health Hazards Not Otherwise Classified 8.1 Labelling Pictogram for Hazards Not Otherwise Classified, Physical Hazards Not Otherwise Classified and Health Hazards Not Otherwise Classified
- 8.1 Labelling Pictogram for Hazards Not Otherwise Classified, Physical Hazards Not Otherwise Classified and Health Hazards Not Otherwise Classified
- Appendix A - Glossary
- Appendix B - References
Published: 2019/07/31
